content:
Let $r$ be the radius of $C_{1}$, and set $O=(0,0)$, $O_{1}=(9-r,0)$ so that $C_{1}$ is tangent internally to $C_{2}:x^{2}+y^{2}=9^{2}$ at $A=(9,0)$.  A point $P$ on $C_{1}$ whose tangent passes through $O$ satisfies 
$$OP^{2}=(O_{1}O)^{2}-r^{2}=(9-r)^{2}-r^{2}=81-18r\,:$$ 
hence 
$$ OP=\sqrt{81-18r}\,. $$ 
Call this length $t=\sqrt{81-18r}$.  Then the ray $OP$ meets $C_{2}$ again at 
$$Q,\quad OQ=9,\;OP=t\;\Longrightarrow\;PQ=9-t.$$ 
The tangent to $C_{1}$ at $A$ is the vertical line $x=9$, which meets the line $OP$ in a point $R$ with 
$$OR=\frac{9}{x_P}\,OP,\quad x_P=\text{the $x$‐coordinate of $P$}.$$ 
A direct computation shows 
$$x_P=\frac{18\,t^{2}}{81+t^{2}}\,,\qquad OR=\frac{9t}{x_P}\,,\quad OQ=9,$$ 
so 
$$QR=OR-OQ=\frac{9t}{x_P}-9\,. $$ 
Setting $PQ=QR$ gives the equation
\[
9-t=\frac{9t}{x_P}-9
\;\Longrightarrow\;
3t^2-36t+81=0
\;\Longrightarrow\;
t=3\text{ or }9.
\]
The nondegenerate solution is $t=3$.  Hence 
\[
\boxed{OP=3}.
\]

message.content:
